strategy_dict.raw_source_excerpt:
THE  COMPLETE  SWING  TRADING  FRAMEWORK 
For  Indian  Equity  Markets  (NSE/BSE) 
Research-Backed  |  Rule-Based  |  SEBI-Aware 
Prepared  for:  Investors  Way  Date:  17  April  2026 
Educational  content  only.  Not  investment  advice.  Author  is  not  a  SEBI-registered  Investment  Adviser  or  Research  Analyst.

1.  Executive  Summary  
This  framework  delivers  a  complete  end-to-end  swing  trading  system  for  Indian  equity  markets,  covering  holding  
periods
 
of
 
2-10
 
days.
 
Every
 
strategy
 
recommended
 
here
 
is
 
backed
 
by
 
at
 
least
 
one
 
of:
 
(a)
 
peer-reviewed
 
academic
 
research,
 
(b)
 
published
 
institutional
 
backtest
 
with
 
disclosed
 
methodology,
 
or
 
(c)
 
publicly
 
verifiable
 
index-level
 
performance
 
data
 
from
 
NSE.
 A  critical  note  upfront:  the  original  brief  asked  for  both  'swing  trading'  and  'intraday  strategies  with  maximum  
winning
 
percentage'
 
in
 
the
 
same
 
request.
 
These
 
are
 
different
 
game
 
entirely
 
—
 
swing
 
trades
 
hold
 
2-10
 
days,
 
intraday
 
closes
 
same-day.
 
This
 
document
 
delivers
 
SWING
 
trading
 
as
 
the
 
primary
 
deliverable.
 
A
 
short
 
intraday
 
appendix
 
is
 
included
 
at
 
the
 
end,
 
but
 
the
 
rigorous
 
backtested
 
evidence
 
across
 
India
 
concentrates
 
in
 
the
 
medium-term
 
momentum
 
space.
 
Key  findings  from  10+  sources  reviewed  •  Momentum  works  in  India.  The  NIFTY  200  Momentum  30  Index  (live  +  backtested  since  April  2005)  has  
delivered
 
roughly
 
19.3%
 
CAGR
 
versus
 
14.3%
 
for
 
the
 
NIFTY
 
200
 
TRI
 
—
 
a
 
~5%
 
annual
 
alpha
 
over
 
20
 
years.
 
This
 
is
 
the
 
single
 
strongest
 
rule-based,
 
verifiable
 
evidence
 
of
 
an
 
edge
 
in
 
Indian
 
equities.
 •  Rebalancing  matters  more  than  picking.  Raju  &  Chandrasekaran  (2019,  SSRN)  show  momentum  'fades'  
within
 
3
 
months.
 
Monthly
 
or
 
bi-weekly
 
rebalancing
 
is
 
the
 
engine
 
that
 
captures
 
the
 
premium.
 •  Be  suspicious  of  80%+  win  rates.  Genuine  swing  strategies  on  liquid  Indian  stocks  cluster  at  45-60%  win  
rates
 
with
 
1.5-2.5:1
 
reward-to-risk
 
ratios.
 
Anything
 
claiming
 
91%
 
win
 
rate
 
(seen
 
in
 
several
 
blog
 
posts)
 
is
 
either
 
curve-fit,
 
uses
 
asymmetric
 
SL,
 
or
 
cherry-picked.
 •  Costs  are  the  silent  killer.  Slippage  of  4-10  bps  per  trade  can  flip  a  marginal  strategy  from  profitable  to  
losing.
 
STCG
 
at
 
15%
 
further
 
reduces
 
net
 
returns.
 •  SEBI  is  tightening  the  rules.  In  December  2025,  SEBI  announced  PaRRVA  (Past  Risk  and  Return  
Verification
 
Agency)
 
and
 
moved
 
to
 
bar
 
live
 
market
 
data
 
in
 
investor-education
 
content.
 
Any
 
public
 
content
 
built
 
on
 
this
 
framework
 
must
 
stay
 
on
 
the
 
education
 
side
 
of
 
the
 
line
 
and
 
carry
 
proper
 
disclaimers.

2.  Top  3  Strategies  —  Comparison  Table  
Ranked  by  risk-adjusted  evidence  quality,  not  by  highest  raw  returns.  All  three  are  long-only  (no  leverage)  and  
designed
 
for
 
swing/positional
 
timeframes.
 
The
 
first
 
is
 
the
 
'core',
 
the
 
second
 
is
 
the
 
'opportunistic
 
breakout
 
overlay',
 
the
 
third
 
is
 
a
 
'mean-reversion
 
hedge'
 
that
 
works
 
in
 
ranging
 
markets.
 
Metric 1.  NIFTY  200  Momentum  30  (Rule-Based) 
2.  52-Week  High  Breakout  +  Volume 
3.  Mean-Reversion  Pullback  to  20-DMA 
Best  market  Trending  (Nifty  above  50-DMA)  
Strongly  trending  +  low  VIX  Ranging  /  post-correction  
Hold  period  2-6  weeks  (until  rebalance)  3-15  trading  days  3-10  trading  days  
Evidence  quality  ★★★★★  NSE  index  data,  20-yr  live+backtest  
★★★★  Academic  support  (Raju  2019)  +  long-standing  Darvas/O'Neil  methodology  
★★★  Concept  well-documented;  Indian-specific  backtests  mostly  anecdotal
